page: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 CPU model operating at 2.7 GHz. The system contains 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware included on this instance type. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression all scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with cached small-block read-only and uncached large-block read/write operations placing in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and gzip single-threaded compression scoring in the average middle 50% range.

The server exhibits a notable performance tradeoff in text generation tasks. While prompt processing for a medium 7B model is strong, text generation for a small 135M model falls into the bottom 10% category. This server is suited for highly parallelized workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, database operations, and static web serving, where it achieves top-tier throughput. It offers high resource density for multi-threaded applications but is less optimal for workloads dependent on high single-core speed or local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture. Powered by AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the c8g.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though its single-core performance remains in the average tier. It exhibits a performance tradeoff in LLM text generation, where speeds for small models fall into the bottom 10%. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized arm64 server with 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-48xl is an arm64 bare-metal server with 192 dedicated cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A bare-metal arm64 server featuring 192 dedicated cores and DDR5 memory, optimized for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical arm64 cores powered by AWS Neoverse-V2 processors.
384.0 GB of DDR5 memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage.
Top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations.
Average single-core performance and poor low-parameter LLM text generation.
Suited for static web serving, parallel processing, and multi-threaded workloads.
